metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2094
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.1: "Scrib1 Interacts and Colocalizes with GluN2 Subunits"

Fig.3: "Modification of Scrib1 Levels Affects Surface Expression of Endogenous GluN2A and GluN2B-Containing Receptors"
Scrib1 is shown to regulate postsynaptic membrane neurotransmitter receptor levels.

Fig.S4: "Scrib1 Interacts with Tac-GluN2A and Tac-GluN2B Chimeras at the Plasma Membrane and in Endosomes of COS-7 Cells"

Fig.5: "Scrib1 Promotes GluN2A Recycling and Inhibits GluN2B Recycling upon Stimulation in Neuron Culture"
term: 'neurotransmitter receptor transport, endosome to postsynaptic membrane (GO:0098887)'

Fig.7: "Scrib1 Regulates the Level of Synaptic NMDARs at Schaffer Collaterals CA1 Pyramidal Cells Synapse"
Demonstrates in vivo (slice recording) relevance for the in vitro (cell culture) observations.

The term 'recycling' is the ensome-to-plasma membrane transport of the NTR

Overall: Experiments show that Scrib1 regulates the internalization and recycling of GluN2A and Glun2B in a differential manner depending on the activity state of the neuron.. Experimental description: - Cell culture of rat species: "All rats were Sprague-Dawley between embryonic day 18 (E18) and 10 weeks old at the time of experiments..."
- Overexpressed protein is of human orgin: "Full-length human Scribble, cloned in pEGFP-C1, was generously provided by Prof. Ian Macara (UVA,
Charlottesville, VA, USA)"
- Annotation to rat species is made.
